--- title: "Plot Simulation Results" subtitle: "Introduction to `{ospsuite}`" module: "Introduction to `{ospsuite}`" ID: A06 date: last-modified institute: | ![](../resources/esqlabs.svg){height=100} format: esqlabs-revealjs --- ::: {.r-fit-text} ## **🎯 Goal: Learn how to create plots from Simulation Results using `{ospsuite}`** ::: ::: {.cell} ::: # Plot Simulation Results ## Simulated and Observed Data Comparing simulated and observed data is crucial in modeling and simulation workflow. `{ospsuite}` provides a set of features to handle these data before visualizing them. - `DataSet` to import and store observed data, - `DataCombined` to link observed and simulated data ## DataSet In `{ospsuite}` observed data are stored in `DataSet` objects. Standard format for data coming from: - Manual entry, - `.pkml` files, - Excel files ## Create DataSet in R Each `DataSet` must be **named** when the object is created. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} dataSet <- DataSet$new("Aciclovir") dataSet ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` • Name: Aciclovir • X dimension: Time • X unit: h • Y dimension: Concentration (mass) • Y unit: mg/l • Error type: NULL • Error unit: NULL • Molecular weight: NULL • LLOQ: NULL Meta data: ``` ::: ::: ## Set Values ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} dataSet$setValues( xValues = c(1, 2, 3, 4), yValues = c(0, 0.1, 0.6, 10), yErrorValues = c(0.001, 0.001, 0.1, 1) ) dataSetToTibble(dataSet) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: ## Import DataSet from PKML Files ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} filePath <- system.file( "extdata", "ObsDataAciclovir_1.pkml", package = "ospsuite" ) dataSet <- loadDataSetFromPKML(filePath = filePath) dataSetToTibble(dataSet) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: ## Import DataSet from Excel File Loading observed data from an Excel sheet requires an `ImporterConfiguration` object. `ImporterConfiguration` describes the mapping of the columns and metadata of the Excel sheet. Can be created in PK-Sim or MoBi (`.xml` file) and loaded in R using the `loadDataImporterConfiguration()` function. ## Load Importer Configuration {.scrollable} ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} configurationFilePath <- system.file( "extdata", "dataImporterConfiguration.xml", package = "ospsuite" ) excelFilePath <- system.file( "extdata", "CompiledDataSet.xlsx", package = "ospsuite" ) dataImporterConfiguration <- loadDataImporterConfiguration( configurationFilePath = configurationFilePath ) dataImporterConfiguration ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` • Time column: Time [h] • Time unit: h • Time unit from column: FALSE • Measurement column: Concentration (mass)[ng/ml] • Measurement unit: ng/ml • Measurement unit from column: FALSE • LLOQ column: NULL • Error column: Error [ng/ml] • Error type: ArithmeticStdDev • Error unit: ng/ml • Grouping columns: Study Id, Organ, Compartment, Species, Gender, Molecule, Route, MW, Patient Id, Dose [unit] • Sheets: • Naming pattern: {Source}.{Sheet}.{Study Id}.{Organ}.{Compartment}.{Species}.{Gender}.{Molecule}.{Route}.{Molecular Weight}.{Subject Id}.{Dose} ``` ::: ::: ## Load Data Sets from Excel ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} dataImporterConfiguration$sheets <- "TestSheet_1" dataSets <- loadDataSetsFromExcel( xlsFilePath = excelFilePath, importerConfigurationOrPath = dataImporterConfiguration ) names(dataSets) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "CompiledDataSet.TestSheet_1.ID01.PeripheralVenousBlood.Plasma.Human.Male.Dapagliflozin.iv..Ind1.1 [mg/kg]" [2] "CompiledDataSet.TestSheet_1.ID01.PeripheralVenousBlood.Plasma.Human.Female.Dapagliflozin.iv..Ind2.1 [mg/kg]" [3] "CompiledDataSet.TestSheet_1.ID02.PeripheralVenousBlood.Plasma.Human.Male.Dapagliflozin.iv..Ind1.1 [mg/kg]" [4] "CompiledDataSet.TestSheet_1.ID02.PeripheralVenousBlood.Plasma.Human.Female.Dapagliflozin.iv..Ind2.1 [mg/kg]" ``` ::: ::: ## DataCombined Links simulated and observed data. ✅ Simulated Data + ✅ Observed Data = `DataCombined` ## Create a DataCombined Object ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} myDataCombined <- DataCombined$new() myDataCombined ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ── Datasets and groupings: ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── NULL ``` ::: ::: ## Add simulation results Use the `addSimulationResults()` method. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} sim <- loadSimulation(system.file( "extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite" )) clearOutputs(sim) addOutputs( "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)", sim ) sim_res <- runSimulations(sim) myDataCombined$addSimulationResults( simulationResults = sim_res[[1]] ) ``` ::: . . . ## Add simulation results — Inspect ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} myDataCombined ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ── Datasets and groupings: ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── # A tibble: 1 × 3 name group dataType 1 Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Ve… simulat… ``` ::: ::: ## Add Observed Data Use the `addDataSets()` method. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} filePath <- system.file( "extdata", "ObsDataAciclovir_1.pkml", package = "ospsuite" ) obsData <- loadDataSetFromPKML(filePath = filePath) myDataCombined$addDataSets( obsData ) myDataCombined ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ── Datasets and groupings: ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── # A tibble: 2 × 3 name group dataType 1 Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Ve… simulat… 2 Vergin 1995.Iv observed ``` ::: ::: ## Inspect as Data Frame ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} myDC_df <- myDataCombined$toDataFrame() head(myDC_df) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: . . . ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} unique(myDC_df$name) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)" [2] "Vergin 1995.Iv" ``` ::: ::: ## First Look — Default Plot ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} plotTimeProfile(myDataCombined) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-12-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Rename and Group Simulated and Observed Data ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} myDataCombined <- DataCombined$new() myDataCombined$addSimulationResults( simulationResults = sim_res[[1]], names = "Simulated", groups = "Aciclovir" ) myDataCombined$addDataSets( obsData, names = "Observed (Vergin 1995.Iv)", groups = "Aciclovir" ) myDC_df <- myDataCombined$toDataFrame() ``` ::: ## Rename and Group — Inspect ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} unique(myDC_df$name) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "Simulated" "Observed (Vergin 1995.Iv)" ``` ::: ::: . . . ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} unique(myDC_df$group) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "Aciclovir" ``` ::: ::: . . . ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} plotTimeProfile(myDataCombined) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-16-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Add several Observed Data ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} myDataCombined2 <- DataCombined$new() myDataCombined2$addSimulationResults( simulationResults = sim_res[[1]], names = "Simulated" ) obsData <- lapply( c( "ObsDataAciclovir_1.pkml", "ObsDataAciclovir_2.pkml", "ObsDataAciclovir_3.pkml" ), function(x) { loadDataSetFromPKML(system.file("extdata", x, package = "ospsuite")) } ) # Name the elements of the list according to the names of the loaded `DataSet`. names(obsData) <- lapply(obsData, function(x) x$name) myDataCombined2$addDataSets( dataSets = c(obsData$`Vergin 1995.Iv`, obsData$`Laskin 1982.Group C`), names = c("Observed (Vergin 1995.Iv)", "Observed (Laskin 1982.Group C)"), groups = "Aciclovir" ) myDC_df <- myDataCombined2$toDataFrame() ``` ::: ## Several Observed — Inspect ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} unique(myDC_df$name) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "Simulated" "Observed (Vergin 1995.Iv)" [3] "Observed (Laskin 1982.Group C)" ``` ::: ::: . . . ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} unique(myDC_df$group) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] NA "Aciclovir" ``` ::: ::: ## Several Observed — Plot ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} plotTimeProfile(myDataCombined2) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-20-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Generate Plots `{ospsuite.plots}` provides five plotting functions. All return `ggplot2` objects. - `plotTimeProfile()` — time profiles - `plotPredictedVsObserved()` — goodness of fit - `plotResidualsVsCovariate()` — residuals against time, observed, or predicted - `plotResidualsAsHistogram()` — residuals distribution - `plotQuantileQuantilePlot()` — residuals normality check ::: {.callout-note title="`setDefaults()`"} `ospsuite.plots::setDefaults()` (called in the setup chunk) installs the OSP house style: color palettes, linear/log scales, legend placement, and watermark settings. Call it once per session before plotting. ::: ## Time Profile ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} plotTimeProfile(myDataCombined) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-21-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Time Profile — Log Scale Plasma profiles are typically read on a log scale. Pass `yScale = "log"`. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} plotTimeProfile(myDataCombined, yScale = "log") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-22-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Predicted vs Observed ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} plotPredictedVsObserved(myDataCombined) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-23-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Residuals vs Predicted ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} plotResidualsVsCovariate(myDataCombined, xAxis = "predicted") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-24-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Residuals vs Time ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} plotResidualsVsCovariate(myDataCombined, xAxis = "time") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-25-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Residuals Histogram ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} plotResidualsAsHistogram(myDataCombined) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-26-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Residuals Q-Q Plot ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} plotQuantileQuantilePlot(myDataCombined) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-27-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Customize Plots All plotting functions return `ggplot2` objects, so customization uses the standard `ggplot2` `+` operator. `ggplot2` builds plots in layers: each `+` adds something new (a title, a theme, axis limits, etc.). A quick reference: [ggplot2.tidyverse.org](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/index.html) ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) plotTimeProfile(myDataCombined) + labs( title = "Example: Customize a Plot", subtitle = "Using ggplot2 layers", caption = "Source: Aciclovir data" ) + theme_minimal() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-28-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Multi-Panel with `{patchwork}` `{ospsuite.plots}` returns plain `ggplot2` objects. Combine them with `{patchwork}` operators `+`, `|`, `/`; `|` places plots side by side, `/` stacks them vertically. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(patchwork) timeProfile <- plotTimeProfile(myDataCombined) resVsPred <- plotResidualsVsCovariate(myDataCombined, xAxis = "predicted") resVsTime <- plotResidualsVsCovariate(myDataCombined, xAxis = "time") ((timeProfile | resVsPred) / resVsTime) + plot_annotation(title = "Multiple plots in one figure") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-29-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Save Plots Use `ospsuite.plots::exportPlot()` to write a plot to disk. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} p <- plotTimeProfile(myDataCombined) ospsuite.plots::exportPlot( plotObject = p, filePath = "resources/A6/exportedPlot.png", width = 20, height = NULL, dpi = 300 ) ``` ::: # Exercises ## Run Simulations 1. Load ospsuite and the aciclovir model in two separate simulation objects called `sim` and `sim_100mg` 2. Modify the `Dose` parameter in `sim_100mg` to 100 mg 3. Run the simulations and assign the output to `results` ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Answer"} library(ospsuite) simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) sim_100mg <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) setParameterValuesByPath( simulation = sim_100mg, parameterPaths = "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose", values = 100, units = "mg" ) results <- runSimulations(simulations = list(sim = sim, sim_100mg = sim_100mg)) ``` ::: ## Create a `DataCombined` object 1. Initialize a `DataCombined` object called `myDataCombined`, 2. Load the data set contained in the `ObsDataAciclovir_1.pkml` file, 3. Add the dataset to `myDataCombined` and specify that the data set name is `Observed` and that it belongs to the `Aciclovir` group, ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Answer"} # 1) myDataCombined <- DataCombined$new() # 2) filePath <- system.file( "extdata", "ObsDataAciclovir_1.pkml", package = "ospsuite" ) dataSet <- loadDataSetFromPKML(filePath = filePath) # 3) myDataCombined$addDataSets( dataSet, names = "Observed", groups = "Aciclovir" ) ``` ::: ## Add Simulation Results 1. Add the simulation results to `myDataCombined` and specify that the simulation results name is `Simulated` and that it belongs to the `Aciclovir` group. 2. Plot the time profile for `myDataCombined`. ::: {.callout-tip} The default pkml exposes more than one output path. When passing a single `name`, also pass `quantitiesOrPaths` to restrict to one output — the vector lengths must match. ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Answer"} plasmaPath <- "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)" myDataCombined$addSimulationResults( simulationResults = results[[1]], quantitiesOrPaths = plasmaPath, names = "Simulated", groups = "Aciclovir" ) plotTimeProfile(myDataCombined) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-33-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Update Simulation Results 1. Replace previous simulation results with the results of the `sim_100mg` simulation in `myDataCombined` 2. Plot the time profile and the predicted vs observed plot from `myDataCombined` ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Answer"} myDataCombined$addSimulationResults( simulationResults = results[[2]], quantitiesOrPaths = plasmaPath, names = "Simulated", groups = "Aciclovir" ) plotTimeProfile(myDataCombined) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-34-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Answer"} plotPredictedVsObserved(myDataCombined) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-34-2.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: ::: ## Plot several outputs 1. Clear the default outputs of `sim` and set the following three outputs: - `Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)` - `Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Whole Blood (Peripheral Venous Blood)` - `Organism|Kidney|Plasma|Aciclovir|Concentration` 2. Rerun the simulation 3. Create a new `DataCombined` object called `myDataCombined2` ## Plot several outputs — Answer ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Answer"} # 1) clear default outputs first, then add the three we want clearOutputs(sim) addOutputs( quantitiesOrPaths = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)", simulation = sim ) addOutputs( quantitiesOrPaths = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Whole Blood (Peripheral Venous Blood)", simulation = sim ) addOutputs( quantitiesOrPaths = "Organism|Kidney|Plasma|Aciclovir|Concentration", simulation = sim ) # 2) results2 <- runSimulations(sim) # 3) myDataCombined2 <- DataCombined$new() ``` ::: ## Add Simulation Results and Customize plot 4. Add the simulation results to `myDataCombined2`. The number of names to provide (in a vector) must match the number of `results2[[1]]$allQuantityPaths` elements. 5. Plot the time profile for `myDataCombined2`, restrict the x-axis to 0–60 minutes, and add a title — using `ggplot2` layers on the returned plot. ## Add Simulation Results and Customize plot — Answer ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Answer"} library(ggplot2) # 4) myDataCombined2$addSimulationResults( simulationResults = results2[[1]], names = c("Plasma", "Whole Blood", "Kidney Plasma") ) # 5) plotTimeProfile(myDataCombined2) + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 60)) + labs( title = "Comparison of Aciclovir Concentrations in Different Compartments" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A6-ospsuiteR-plot_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-36-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.8%} ::: :::

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
